is an organisation that promotes indoor [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") and organises competitions (primarily in the United Kingdom), including the [World Indoor Bowls Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [World Bowls Tour Events](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Tour_Events&redirect=no "World Bowls Tour Events"))

Sports organisation

Not to be confused with [World Bowls](/wiki/World_Bowls "World Bowls").

World Bowls Tour

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Abbreviation | WBT |
| Formation | 1997 |
| Headquarters | Galgate, Lancaster, United Kingdom |
| Chairperson (PBA) | [David Bolt](/wiki/David_Bolt_(bowls) "David Bolt (bowls)") |
| Website | [worldbowlstour.tv](https://worldbowlstour.tv/) [worldbowlstour.org](https://worldbowlstour.org/) |

The **World Bowls Tour** is an organisation that promotes indoor [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") and organises competitions (primarily in the United Kingdom), including the [World Indoor Bowls Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Tour&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 1990, the sport was experiencing period of fragmentation following the loss of the television coverage of the [World Indoor Bowls Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships"). John Hall, the President of the [English Bowls Association](/wiki/Bowls_England "Bowls England"), unsuccessfully called for closer co-operation between the indoor and outdoor games.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1992, a player-led organisation formed the Professional Bowls Association, during the [1992 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/1992_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "1992 World Indoor Bowls Championship"), which was held in the [Preston Guildhall](/wiki/Preston_Guildhall "Preston Guildhall") to promote indoor bowls worldwide and on television.

On 1 January 1997, under the then-chairman [Richard Corsie](/wiki/Richard_Corsie "Richard Corsie"), the PBA created the World Bowls Tour and after the [1997 championship](/wiki/1997_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "1997 World Indoor Bowls Championship") replaced the World Indoor Bowls Council as the leading indoor organisation.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The purpose of the World Bowls Tour was to create championships and competitions to further the aims of the PBA.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Tour&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Structure")]

| Position | Name |
| --- | --- |
| Chairperson | [David Bolt](/wiki/David_Bolt_(bowls) "David Bolt (bowls)") |
| Tournament Director | [Tom Weir](/wiki/Tom_Weir "Tom Weir") |
| Assistant Tournament Director & Finance Director | Martin Gale |
| Corporate Director | Jason Parkinson |

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Tour&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards")]

* [World Bowls Tour Awards](/wiki/World_Bowls_Tour_Awards "World Bowls Tour Awards")

## Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Tour&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Events")]

Current and former indoor bowls competitions organised by the World Bowls Tour.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### World Indoor Bowls Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Tour&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: World Indoor Bowls Championships")]

Main article: [World Indoor Bowls Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships")

### Youngest player

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_Bowls_Tour&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Youngest player")]

The youngest player is Desmond Lai from Hong Kong, China, who at the age of 15 (January 2024) took part in the 2024 World Bowls Indoor Championships.[[33]](#cite_note-33)
